extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_a is set true only by r1 (diabetes AND peptic_ulcer AND renal_impairment). rec_drug_b is set true by r2 (diabetes). These rules target different outputs, so there is no priority conflict to suppress either; each writes its own output. Whenever r1's condition holds, diabetes is necessarily true, so r2's condition also holds. With diabetes=true, peptic_ulcer=true, renal_impairment=true, r1 fires setting rec_drug_a:=true and r2 fires setting rec_drug_b:=true. Defaults only apply to unset outputs, so they do not intervene. Thus both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are true simultaneously, violating the mutual-exclusion property. hyperkalemia is irrelevant (set false); r3 only affects rec_drug_c.
